summary: High‑energy 1987 Utah show shines with stellar playing despite a flawed matrix mix that mutes Bobby’s guitar.
review: The performance is praised as “incredible” with standout moments on Minglewood Blues, Cassidy, Stella Blue and the entire second set.  The UltraMatrix source captures a clear soundboard signal but suffers from poor mixes – missing much of Bobby’s guitar and occasional clipping – requiring a pitch correction for the second set.  Overall fans love the music and consider it a hidden gem even if the recording isn’t perfect.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is rated very high (stellar playing, many standout songs) while recording quality is only fair due to mix problems; the weighted average yields a ~4‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Original UltraMatrix combines SBD and FM sources; clear soundboard but bad mix (no Bobby guitar in many sections), over‑PA monitoring, occasional clipping, needs -1% pitch correction for second set.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds a strong sequence with high‑level jams; second set is “meaty” with six songs before and after the drum solo, delivering consistently high energy despite occasional mix frustrations.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar thrives on Minglewood Blues and delivers moments of incredulity throughout the set; his tone is praised even though the mix often drowns it out.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s bass work is highlighted as a joy on Row Jimmy and he provides powerful intros (Whole Lotta Love) and strong playing on The Wheel.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s guitar is frequently missing or turned down in the mix, with occasional monotone shouting on Sugar Mag; when audible his slide work adds nastiness that makes Jerry thrive.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland is at his peak, sounding “like Keith” on Push Comes to Shove and delivering strong keyboards throughout the second set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Intense drum performance with lots of gear for an outdoor show; the drums segment is described as “intense” and worth listening to.
